rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 28, 2018 line 1. Defendant'S Motion For Protective Order GRANTED. Responses to discovery to be provided no later than January 15, 2019. The Court finds that Plaintiff's unwillingness to provide routine courtesy to allow Defendant to timely respond to four sets of voluminous discovery under the circumstances of this case was entirely unreasonable, not in good faith, and constitutes abuse of discovery process. Sanctions granted in the amount of $10,200 against Plaintiff and his Counsel. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Edmund Chute'S Motion For Temporary Protective Order, Permanent Protective Order.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 28, 2018 line 1. Defendant Edmund Chute'S Motion For Temporary Protective Order, Permanent Protective Order OFF CALENDAR, duplicate entry. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES JONES VS. ESTATE OF JOSIE STAMPS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 28, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT DEBRA DOLCH's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Plaintiff was provided an opportunity to allege any cause of action against the moving defendant. Based on the allegations in the operative complaint no cause of action may be ascertained. Moving party and its counsel are ordered to comply with CRC 3.1110 and SFLR 2.7B (courtesy copy of the operative complaint) in future. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES JONES VS. ESTATE OF JOSIE STAMPS ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 28, 2018 line 2. Plaintiff was provided an opportunity to allege any cause of action against the moving defendant. Based on the allegations in the operative complaint no cause of action may be ascertained. = (501/CFH)
